query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1; live-reproduced on 8792: n=1 returned RemoveField.deconstruct@0.7904, base Field.deconstruct absent from top-5. Symbol identity is not a ranking signal.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.7: forced-inclusion of exact symbol-name matches into hybrid pool; Field.deconstruct now rank1 score=1.2. UPG-11.10: symbol displayed as qualified 'Field.deconstruct'.

query: from_db_value convert database value to python object on a model field
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1; query for from_db_value surfaced sibling get_db_prep_value. CORRECTED (F7/loop1 gate): base Field in fields/__init__.py has NO from_db_value method; the real implementation is JSONField.from_db_value at json.py:87. Expectation updated to target the correct file. UPDATED loop2: UPG-11.10 changed symbol display from bare 'from_db_value' to qualified 'JSONField.from_db_value'; updated expect.symbol accordingly. Actual rank3 now matches.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12: JSONField.from_db_value now rank3 score=0.9971. AreaField.from_db_value (rank1, GIS) and DistanceField.from_db_value (rank2, GIS) outrank it but all three are valid from_db_value impls. Top-3 contains JSONField.from_db_value in json.py.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F1 sub-bug: displayed score is the STALE pre-rerank hybrid score; results not ordered by it (rank1=0.780 < rank5=0.798). Ship a real, monotonic ranking score regardless of F1.1.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.2: score is now the final post-rerank composite score; verified monotonic [1.2, 1.0124, 1.0051, 0.9978, 0.9905].

query: how to write a custom model field with deconstruct and from_db_value
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F2: 8792 indexed python/html/javascript/markdown ONLY. Django ships docs as .txt; docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt (the single best resource for the task) was invisible to search. Opus D found it via Glob+Read.
resolved: loop4 UPG-11.11: is_doc_intent_query() classifies 'how to ...' queries; on doc-intent, forced-inclusion suppressed AND doc-prose quality multiplier raised to 1.0 (from 0.70). docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt surfaces in top-5. F1/F1b/F4/F5/F7/F8 code-intent queries unregressed (forced-inclusion still active, doc-prose still 0.70).

query: understand the base Field class lifecycle and validation
origin: eval-v2 N=1 audit F3: comprehension queries return a 2-5k chunk; model re-read the 12-14k fields/__init__.py 4-8x/session because search offers no expand-to-full-symbol/file affordance. Encode as metric once the affordance exists; currently a known gap.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.4: symbol_start_line and symbol_end_line now present in /v1/search REST response. Verified: rank1 returns symbol_start_line=73 symbol_end_line=134.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: indexer stores symbol_name as bare leaf only (e.g. 'deconstruct', never 'Field.deconstruct'). The symbol_identity_boost qualified-match path (+0.20 for Class.method) is dead code at query time because re.split('[.:]+', symbol_name) always yields a single-element list. All deconstruct overrides get identical +0.10 leaf boost; discrimination is impossible. Fix: store symbol_name as 'Class.method' (using the class_context already tracked in _collect_chunks_ast at indexer.py:306) OR extract class prefix from content '# class: X' at query time in _apply_quality_and_dedup.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.10: symbol_name now stored/displayed as 'Class.method'; UPG-11.7 forced-inclusion lifts Field.deconstruct to rank1.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: Field.deconstruct (lines 606-705, ~100 lines with docstring) does not appear in the top-50 hybrid candidate pool for the F1 query. The boost is applied post-retrieval and cannot rescue a chunk that never entered the pool. Root cause: the long docstring diverges in embedding space from the short concrete query; BM25 scores are also low because keyword overlap is diluted. Fix options: (1) index the method signature+first-line as a separate short chunk, (2) expand vector fetch pool beyond _RERANK_TOP_K_UNFILTERED=60, (3) store symbol metadata for forced inclusion of exact-name matches.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.7: forced-inclusion unions all chunks whose symbol leaf matches a query token into the pool before rerank. Field.deconstruct now rank1.

query: list all database migrations for a project
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: symbol_identity_boost fires a +0.10 leaf boost for any symbol whose leaf is a common English word that appears in the query. Examples: leaf='all' boosted by 'list all migrations'; leaf='get' boosted by 'get a record'; leaf='set' boosted by 'set field value'. is_compound=False for single-word 3+ char leaves, so sub-word path fires on casual English use. No minimum-length guard or stop-word filter. Impact: can silently shift rankings on any query containing common English words that coincide with short method names.
resolved: loop1 f35dee1: stop-word + min-leaf-len(4) guard in symbol_identity_boost; verified GREEN over REST on django ('all' absent from top-5). Note: 4-letter gap tracked separately as F8. Note loop2: forced-inclusion now pulls LinearGeometryMixin.project to rank1 via 'project' (7 chars); 'all' absent check still passes but a NEW fault exists (tracked as F9).

query: from_db_value convert database value to python object on a model field
origin: loop1 UPG-11.1 gate: F1b expects 'Field.from_db_value' but the base Field class in django/db/models/fields/__init__.py has NO from_db_value method. The method only exists on JSONField (json.py:87), GIS fields (gis/db/models/sql/conversion.py), etc. F1b's expected file+symbol are wrong; the correct expectation is top-3 contains JSONField.from_db_value or similar concrete implementation. Actual rank1 is DatabaseOperations.convert_datefield_value (oracle) which is semantically unrelated. Both the test expectation and the ranking are wrong. UPDATED loop2: UPG-11.10 changed display to qualified form; corrected expected symbol to 'JSONField.from_db_value'. JSONField.from_db_value is now at rank3 (json.py) but harness checks top-3 WITH combined file+symbol. Passes the relaxed check but AreaField/DistanceField (GIS) outrank JSONField.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12: JSONField.from_db_value confirmed at rank3 score=0.9971 with file=json.py. Top-3 check passes. GIS fields outrank but are semantically valid implementations.

query: read lines from a text file
origin: loop2 adversarial: stop-word guard covers 3-letter-and-under leaves and a curated set of English stopwords, but 4-letter common English/programming words (read, join, hash, repr, open, eval, exec, iter, next, sort, copy, load, dump, emit, bind, call, send, wait) are NOT in prog_stopwords.txt and have len>=SYMBOL_MIN_LEAF_LEN=4. When these appear as natural-language verbs in a query (e.g. 'read lines from a text file' contains token 'read'), any symbol with leaf='read' gets +0.10 spurious boost. Confirmed: symbol_identity_boost('read', tokens)=0.1 for query 'read lines from a text file'. Impact: latent risk in code-heavy corpora (uv/rust/zig) where 'read', 'send', 'join', 'call' are common method names that compete with more relevant symbols. Django corpus shows the read method at rank7 with score 0.7106 boosted by +0.10 on this query.
resolved: loop2 UPG-11.8: 'read' added to prog_stopwords.txt; verified 'read' leaf absent from top-3. rank1=HttpRequest.readlines (leaf='readlines', not 'read') which is correct behavior.

query: list all database migrations for a project
origin: loop2 adversarial: UPG-11.7 forced-inclusion uses _FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7 as the guard for non-compound tokens. Common English words of 7+ chars that also appear as method names cause forced-inclusion false positives. Example: 'project' (7 chars, no underscore) in 'list all database migrations for a project' triggers forced-inclusion of LinearGeometryMixin.project from django.contrib.gis.geos.geometry into the candidate pool. It receives sym_boost=+0.10 and final score=1.1 (rank1), beating Migration (rank2, 0.9344) and migration docs. Root cause: the 7-char threshold correctly targets identifiers like 'deconstruct' (11) and 'migrations' (10) but also captures prose words like 'project', 'message', 'execute', 'context', 'setting', 'response', 'process'. Impact: any query containing a common English word of 7+ chars that coincides with a method name risks having an unrelated symbol at rank1.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): BM25+vec-sim relevance gate for non-compound tokens. LinearGeometryMixin.project ABSENT from top-3; rank1=Migration score=0.95, rank2=Migration score=0.7833, rank3=docs/topics/migrations.txt score=0.7. Gate works: geometry chunk has near-zero BM25 overlap with migration/database terms minus 'project'.

query: connect to database and execute query
origin: loop2 adversarial: 'execute' (7 chars, no underscore) triggers forced-inclusion, pulling ALL .execute() methods into the pool with sym_boost. This floods top-4: BaseCommand.execute (rank1), ManagementUtility.execute (rank2), Command.execute sqlmigrate (rank3), Command.execute runserver (rank4). CursorWrapper.execute (the correct DB cursor answer) appears at rank7 only. Additionally 'connect' (7 chars) forces Signal.connect to rank5. The correct answer is demoted 6 ranks by an unrelated method-name collision.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): CursorWrapper.execute is rank1 score=1.1; BaseDatabaseWrapper.connect rank2 score=1.0841; BaseDatabaseSchemaEditor.execute rank3 score=1.0683. Both 'execute' and 'connect' candidates pass the relevance gate because db/backends chunks have strong BM25 overlap with 'database', 'query', 'connect' terms.

query: render template context in view
origin: loop2 adversarial: 'context' (7 chars) and 'template' (8 chars) both trigger forced-inclusion. DecimalField.context (a cached_property for decimal precision context) gets sym_boost=+0.10 and final score=1.1, landing at rank1 for 'render template context in view'. The correct answers (RequestContext, TemplateResponse, render_to_string) are absent from top-3. Root cause: same as F9 — the 7-char non-compound threshold captures common English words.
resolved: loop3 UPG-11.12 (commits e6bf165+c968f5e): DecimalField.context ABSENT from top-3; rank1=PartialTemplate.render score=1.1, rank2=BaseRenderer.render score=1.0833, rank3=TemplateView.get score=0.9667. Decimal context chunk fails relevance gate: near-zero BM25 overlap with 'render','template','view' minus 'context'.

query: Field deconstruct base class name path args kwargs migration
origin: loop2 adversarial: displayed scores regularly exceed 1.0 (e.g., Field.deconstruct=1.2, BloomIndex.deconstruct=1.0124). The scoring formula is base_rank(1.0 for rank1) * quality + sym_boost(0.1 or 0.2), which is not bounded at [0,1]. A user seeing score=1.2 has no natural interpretation — scores are supposed to indicate relevance similarity. This is an ergonomics/UX issue: the score field convention from vector similarity [0,1] is violated. Impact: callers who use score as a threshold (e.g., 'only use if score>0.8') get false signals; scores are monotonic but unnormalized. Note: sorted_by_score remains true so ranking correctness is unaffected; this is purely a display/contract issue.

query: save a model instance to the database
origin: loop3 adversarial false-negative attack: 'save' is 4 chars, no underscore — excluded from forced-inclusion (correct by min_identifier_len=7 guard). Model.save in django/db/models/base.py:841 does NOT appear in top-50 results for any natural-language formulation of 'save a model'. Rank1 is always ModelAdmin.save_model (admin helper, not the ORM method) because save_model is a compound name that semantically matches 'save'+'model' well in the embedding space. Model.save only surfaces when queried with code-syntax tokens like 'force_insert force_update update_fields'. User impact: a developer asking how to save a model instance in Django will be directed to the admin helper rather than the core ORM method — the single most-used Django API is invisible to natural-language search. Root cause: 4-char common verbs ('save','get','set','add') below forced-inclusion threshold AND lose the regular hybrid to compound-named admin variants that score higher on vector similarity. This is a pre-existing limitation not introduced by UPG-11.12.

query: exclude certain records from a queryset
origin: loop3 adversarial: 'queryset' (8 chars, not a stop word) passes the forced-inclusion guard and force-includes ALL methods named 'queryset' (10 ListFilter.queryset() variants in django). These pass the BM25+vec-sim relevance gate because filter-context chunks have some keyword overlap. Result: ListFilter.queryset ranks1-3 (scores 1.013, 0.9986, 0.9841), QuerySet.exclude is demoted to rank4 (score 0.9826). The word 'queryset' in the query is used as a NOUN (the container) but treated by forced-inclusion as a method-name target. Same pattern as F9/F11 but the relevance gate fails to exclude ListFilter.queryset because those chunks DO discuss filtering querysets (BM25/vec-sim both pass). Root cause: 'queryset' should be in the forced-inclusion prose stop-word list alongside 'context', 'project' etc. Impact: any natural-language query using 'queryset' as a noun (very common in Django queries) risks having the genuinely correct ORM method (exclude, filter, values, annotate) pushed off rank1-3 by ListFilter admin variants.

query: login password
origin: loop4 adversarial: 2-word query 'login password' returns Style (rank1, score=0.6), NewsArticle (rank2, score=0.4). Both are 'class Foo(Bar): pass' or 'class Foo: pass' stub definitions with NO body. is_trivial_chunk() returns False for these because it only triggers trivial when there is exactly 1 meaningful line; a 2-line stub (class decl + pass) has 2 meaningful lines after _meaningful_lines(). quality_score returns 0.8 for the class decl + 0.44 for subclass. These pass-body stubs have ZERO retrieval value but score 0.8 and rank above all auth-related code on a 2-token query. Root cause: is_trivial_chunk does not handle the (class_declaration + pass) = 2-line stub pattern. Impact: ultra-short queries (2-3 tokens) return empty stubs as top results; the model has no signal to filter them. Severity: HIGH — ultra-short queries are common from IDE tools calling vectr with symbol-only lookups.

query: email send message backend smtp
origin: loop4 adversarial: 5-word professional query 'email send message backend smtp' returns i18n_catalog.js (rank1, score=0.79), HttpResponseServerError (rank2, score=0.6), jsi18n-mocks.test.js (rank3), HttpResponseBadRequest (rank4). EmailMessage.send (the correct answer, django/core/mail/message.py) is absent from top-10. The doc 'docs/topics/email.txt' appears only at rank5 with score=0.44. Root cause: 'send' is 4 chars (excluded from short-verb allowlist), 'email' is 5 chars (below FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7, no underscore), 'backend' is 7 chars but does NOT appear as a method name so no FI. The vector embedding of 'email send message backend smtp' apparently has cosine similarity to i18n_catalog.js > EmailMessage.send — a vector model quality issue compounded by lack of BM25 keyword overlap rescue. Separately, 'smtp' and 'backend' are 7-char+ but aren't method names in index. No forced-inclusion fires. Impact: common, professional-phrasing queries for Django email features return JavaScript test stubs.

query: ForeignKey on_delete CASCADE related_name
origin: loop4 adversarial: query for ForeignKey on_delete behavior returns DatabaseCache.delete (rank1), RedisCacheClient.delete (rank2), RedisCache.delete (rank3), LocMemCache.delete (rank4), BaseCache.delete (rank5). All cache backends. The relevant ForeignKey/RelatedField code is absent. Root cause: 'delete' is in FORCED_INCLUSION_SHORT_VERB_ALLOWLIST; it is extracted as a sub-token from 'on_delete' by _query_symbol_tokens, triggering forced-inclusion of all .delete() methods. Cache backends pass the BM25+vec-sim relevance gate because they share 'delete', 'key', 'related' etc with the query. The compound token 'on_delete' also fires unconditionally, but cache delete chunks dominate because they get both on_delete and delete boosts. Any query containing 'on_delete' as a sub-word of a longer compound causes delete() methods to flood results. Impact: ForeignKey/relationship queries are a top Django developer use-case and ALL return wrong results.

query: how to write a custom model field with deconstruct and from_db_value
origin: loop4 regression: F2 was marked passing in loop3 but now FAILS on current branch. docs/howto/custom-model-fields.txt appears at rank10 (score=0.817), not top-5. is_doc_intent=True fires correctly, DOC_INTENT_SUPPRESS_FORCED_INCLUSION=True fires correctly. But DOC_INTENT_DOC_PROSE_MULTIPLIER=1.0 means doc score = raw * 1.0 (no boost, just 'no penalty'). Code chunks with high vector similarity (Field.deconstruct score=1.0) still beat the doc. Root cause: the multiplier for doc-intent needs to be > 1.0 (a genuine BOOST) for docs to compete with code that has strong exact-symbol matches AND forced-inclusion. 1.0 removes the penalty but doesn't provide the promotion needed. Severity: REGRESSION — this was passing in loop3.

query: session expired logout
origin: loop5 adversarial: query 'session expired logout' returns ranks 1-10 as: (1) SessionStore stub (4-line class+pass, score=0.55), (2-10) all 1-line HTML/TXT test fixtures ('django', 'Logged out', 'from-my-custom-list', 'Oh dear, an error occurred!', 'Password reset successfully', 'file1 in the app dir', 'File in otherdir.', 'Oh no, an error occurred!', 'Archive for {{ week }}.'). The real session backend (SessionBase.clear_expired, SessionStore.clear_expired in contrib/sessions/) is completely absent from top-10. Same pattern confirmed on 'error occurred' (all 5 results are 1-line HTML templates) and 'permission denied user authentication' (ranks 3-8 are 1-line fixtures). Root cause: is_trivial_chunk() does not recognize 1-line prose strings in HTML/TXT files as trivial — 'Logged out', 'Password reset successfully', 'oracledb >= 2.3.0' all pass is_trivial=False and get quality_score=0.8 (same as a real method body). 161+ such files in tests/templates/ and tests/requirements/ are fully indexed with full quality weight. When a short/prose query has even moderate vector similarity to these strings, they outrank all real code. Impact: any short 2-3 word query whose words appear in test template fixtures returns zero actionable results — the model has nothing to act on. Severity: HIGH — short queries and error-recovery queries (common in IDE use) are completely broken.

query: configure Django settings for production deployment
origin: loop5 adversarial: query 'configure Django settings for production deployment' returns MailersHandler.settings (rank1, score=1.0) and BaseConnectionHandler.settings (rank2, score=1.0) via forced-inclusion. Root cause: 'settings' (8 chars, no underscore) passes all forced-inclusion guards — above _FORCED_INCLUSION_MIN_IDENTIFIER_LEN=7, and (unlike singular 'setting') not in prog_stopwords.txt. Fixed in UPG-15.6 by adding 'settings' to prog_stopwords.txt. ASSERTION CORRECTED (sentinel + product-reviewer, 2026-06-22): the original positive expectation (docs/topics/settings.txt in top-5) was aspirational/wrong — with the false-positive removed, the genuinely-correct rank-1 is LazySettings.configure (the canonical 'configure settings' symbol), and the deployment-specific docs that follow are legitimately more relevant to 'production deployment' than the generic settings reference (settings.txt at rank10 is correct, not a bug). The real defect was the MailersHandler.settings false positive (negative assertion), which is fixed. Positive assertion now checks LazySettings.configure in top-5.

query: read lines from a text file
origin: loop6 regression: UPG-15.5 raised top_k_unfiltered from 60 to 200. This widens the cross-encoder candidate pool. StreamingBuffer.read (django/utils/text.py lines 367-371, a 5-line read() method that resets a BytesIO buffer) now enters the pool at the wider depth and the cross-encoder ranks it at position 2 (score=0.865) for 'read lines from a text file'. The 'read' stopword guard in prog_stopwords.txt correctly prevents forced-inclusion (+0.10 FI boost does NOT fire), but the chunk ranks #2 purely on cross-encoder semantic relevance — the reranker considers a method named 'read' that reads a buffer as directly relevant to the query. This was previously invisible because at top_k=60, StreamingBuffer.read was outside the retrieval pool. F8 was marked 'resolved' (UPG-11.8) but is now failing again due to the pool size increase. Execute_from_command_line (rank1, score=0.900) is also wrong — neither result is relevant to 'read lines from a text file'. Root cause: raising top_k_unfiltered to 200 pulls semantically-proximate-but-wrong chunks into the pool that the cross-encoder finds plausible. The stopword guard blocks FI but cannot block semantic ranking. Fix direction: UPG-15.7 (pool-entry trivial filter before cross-encoder) or a more granular per-query pool size based on query semantic confidence.

query: delete rows from database table
origin: loop6 adversarial: standalone 'delete' in FORCED_INCLUSION_SHORT_VERB_ALLOWLIST fires FI for ALL symbols with leaf='delete'. Query 'delete rows from database table' returns RedisCacheClient.delete (rank1), RedisCache.delete (rank2), LocMemCache.delete (rank3), BaseCache.delete (rank4), BaseMemcachedCache.delete (rank5), all score=1.000. QuerySet.delete and Model.delete are absent from top-12. The BM25-without-trigger and vec_sim_floor=0.52 relevance gates do not filter cache.delete() chunks because 'delete' as the trigger token dominates BM25, and the cross-encoder gives cache.delete code a cosine sim >0.52 to the full query (cache backends' delete() methods share structural resemblance to DB row deletion). This is the standalone-'delete' variant of F17 (which was about on_delete as a compound). Root cause: 'delete' in the short-verb allowlist is semantically too broad — it matches cache.delete, storage.delete, session.delete as readily as Model/QuerySet.delete. The relevance gate (BM25 floor=0.05 + vec_sim_floor=0.52) is too permissive to distinguish 'database table' context from 'cache key' context. User impact: 'delete rows/records/objects from database' is a top developer use-case and returns exclusively cache internals — HIGH severity.

query: get object by primary key
origin: loop6 adversarial: standalone 'get' in FORCED_INCLUSION_SHORT_VERB_ALLOWLIST fires FI for all symbols with leaf='get'. Query 'get object by primary key' returns RedisCacheClient.get (rank1), RedisCache.get (rank2), LocMemCache.get (rank3), BaseCache.get (rank4), BaseMemcachedCache.get (rank5), all score=1.000. QuerySet.get and get_object_or_404 are absent from top-5. The correct answer (QuerySet.get at django/db/models/query.py) is displaced by cache backend .get() methods via forced-inclusion flooding. Same root cause as F22 (short-verb FI allowlist too broad). Related to F17 (on_delete sub-token causing delete flooding) but manifests on the standalone 'get' verb. User impact: 'get object by pk', 'get by id', 'retrieve single record' queries return cache internals — HIGH severity since these are the most common ORM lookup patterns.

query: how to create a custom management command
origin: loop7 adversarial: doc-intent query returns docs/howto/index.txt (rank1, a TOC file listing custom-management-commands.txt but NOT it) and Django.egg-info/SOURCES.txt (rank2, score=1.000, a build-artifact file manifest with zero educational content). docs/howto/custom-management-commands.txt is absent from top-5. Root cause 1: SOURCES.txt is a plain-text file listing source paths that matches BM25 on identifiers like 'management', 'commands' but contains no content. Root cause 2: the actual howto .txt file is outranked by the TOC and the build artifact. The doc-intent DOC_INTENT_DOC_PROSE_MULTIPLIER applies to all .txt files equally, including build artifacts. User impact: developer asking how to create management commands is shown a file manifest as their second result. HIGH severity.

query: what is a model Meta class
origin: loop7 adversarial: doc-intent 'what is' query returns all 8 results from tests/model_forms/tests.py — all are 3-line inner Meta class stubs (class Meta: / model = Writer / fields = '__all__'). docs/ref/models/options.txt (canonical reference for model Meta options) is absent from top-8. Root cause: inner class stubs 'class Meta: model=X fields=__all__' are 3-line chunks with quality_score ~0.8, with 200+ instances in test files. Embedding similarity to these stubs is HIGH (they literally contain 'class Meta' as the dominant token); the reference doc has lower cosine sim. Doc-intent multiplier did not fire or did not overcome the sheer volume of 3-line test stubs. User impact: developer asking what a Meta class is gets only 3-line test stubs, zero documentation. HIGH severity.

query: view class based generic list create update destroy
origin: loop7 adversarial: query for Django class-based views returns score=1.000 from completely unrelated files: ProgressBar.update (serializers/base.py), Context.update (template/context.py), Node.create (utils/tree.py), MultiValueDict.update (utils/datastructures.py), TranslationCatalog.update (utils/translation/trans_real.py). django/views/generic/ is absent from top-5. Root cause: tokens 'create', 'update', 'destroy', 'list' each trigger forced-inclusion of ALL symbols with those leaf names across the codebase. Cross-encoder then scores these unrelated .update()/.create() methods at 1.000 for a query containing the verb. Multi-verb compound where 4 short verbs each independently flood, identical mechanism to F22/F23 but compound. User impact: CBV queries are a top Django use-case and return only internal utility method stubs. HIGH severity.

query: parse query string url parameters request GET POST data
origin: loop7 adversarial: query for HTTP request parameter parsing returns cache backends exclusively: RedisCacheClient.get (rank1, 0.932), RedisCache.get (rank2, 0.922), BaseMemcachedCache.get (rank3, 0.913), MailersHandler.get (rank4, 0.904), DummyCache.get (rank5, 0.894). django/http/request.py (HttpRequest.GET/POST QueryDict) is absent from top-8. Root cause: 'GET' in the query triggers forced-inclusion of all symbols with leaf='get' (same mechanism as F23). Here 'GET' is an HTTP method name not a Python method verb, but FI cannot distinguish. Distinct from F23 (HTTP method context vs ORM context) but same root mechanism: case-insensitive token 'get' matches .get() method stubs. User impact: HTTP request parsing queries — one of the most common Django patterns — return cache backend stubs. HIGH severity.

query: signals
origin: loop7 adversarial: 1-word query 'signals' returns Django.egg-info/SOURCES.txt at rank4 AND rank5 (two 200-line windows of the file manifest, scores 0.671 and 0.642). django/dispatch/ (the signals framework) is absent from top-5. Result1 is django/db/backends/signals.py (3-line file, minimal). Result2 is an i18n JS catalog function (unrelated). Root cause: SOURCES.txt has multiple 200-line chunks; chunks containing 'signals.py' paths match BM25/vector on 'signals' and pass trivial filter. The file is a build artifact indexed as first-class .txt. Any short query matching a source-path segment ('signals', 'forms', 'models', 'views') will surface SOURCES.txt chunks. Related to F24 (same SOURCES.txt artifact). User impact: single-word concept queries return file manifest chunks in place of framework code. Medium-high severity.

query: Model
origin: loop7 adversarial via vectr_locate: vectr_locate('Model') returned 10 results ALL from test files (tests/check_framework/test_model_checks.py, tests/check_framework/test_model_field_deprecation.py). django/db/models/base.py:Model (the canonical base model class, 1400+ lines) was absent from top-10 locate results. Root cause: vectr_locate exact-name matching returns multiple inner test-scope classes named 'Model' (defined inside test methods as local class Model(models.Model)), with no ranking signal preferring the library-level definition over inner test classes. UPG-15.10 adds scope-depth + span-bucket ranking to vectr_locate so the module-level definition outranks inner test-scope classes. This case asserts directly against /v1/locate via the harness locate-assertion path added in UPG-15.10; the earlier vectr_search proxy was a harness limitation, not the real fault.
